title: packera pseudaurea subsp. pseudaurea
intro: Packera pseudaurea subsp. pseudaurea, also known as false golden ragwort or western butterweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Packera pseudaurea subsp. pseudaurea include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular remedy for various skin conditions and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of Packera pseudaurea subsp. pseudaurea include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
